875	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
876	(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**
877	
878	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
879	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
880	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
881	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.
882	
883	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
884	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
885	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
886	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
887	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
888	and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
889	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
890	`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.
891	
892	Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
893	`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
894	each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
895	(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
896	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.
897	
898	The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
899	category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
900	vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
901	`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
902	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
903	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
904	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
905	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
906	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
907	  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
908	  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
909	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
910	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
911	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
912	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
913	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
914	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
915	  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
916	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
917	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
918	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
919	      (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
920	  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
921	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
922	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
923	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
924	          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
925	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
926	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
927	            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
928	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
929	  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
930	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
931	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
932	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
933	      f.toRingCatSheafHom
934	  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
935	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
936	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
937	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
938	      h.toRingCatSheafHom
939	  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
940	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) ≫
941	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
942	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
943	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
944	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
945	    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
946	        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
947	    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
948	      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
949	      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
950	      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
951	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
952	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
953	    have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
954	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
955	              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
956	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
957	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
958	            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
959	      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left]; exact congrArg _ hLh
960	    exact e.symm.trans key2
961	  have hcomp' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
962	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
963	        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
964	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
965	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
966	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
967	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
968	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
969	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
970	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
971	    have ef := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
972	      (X := SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
973	      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
974	    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app, Functor.comp_map]
975	    erw [Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, hinner, Functor.map_comp]
976	    erw [← Category.assoc]
977	    rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
978	            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
979	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
980	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
981	        = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom from ef.symm.trans hLf]
982	    rfl
983	  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, hi]
984	  erw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.naturality
985	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
986	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)]
987	  erw [← Category.assoc, hcomp']
988	  erw [Category.assoc]
989	
990	/-! ### Phase 1 — `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`
991	
992	**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
993	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
994	schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
995	`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
996	functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
997	preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
998	representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
999	unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
1000	chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
1001	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
1002	retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
1003	use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).
1004	
1005	The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
1006	several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
1007	the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
1008	`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
1009	fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
1010	`Functor.Monoidal.μIso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
1011	`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
1012	resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso … (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
1013	— the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
1014	synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/
1015	
1016	/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
1017	Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
1018	`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
1019	hypotheses (see the section note). -/
1020	private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1021	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1022	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1023	
1024	/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
1025	comparison functor — the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso`. Project-local:
1026	hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
1027	reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/
1028	noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1029	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1030	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1031	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1032	  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
1033	    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)
1034	
1035	@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1036	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1037	    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
1038	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl
1039	
1040	/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
1041	`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X`, where
1042	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
1043	always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
1044	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
1045	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1046	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1047	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1048	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
1049	  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f
1050	
1051	/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
1052	sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
1053	presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
1054	presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
1055	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
1056	monoidal" reconciliation, built — exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — by
1057	whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
1058	on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
1059	with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
1060	presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
1061	sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
1062	`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
1063	private noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
1064	    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1065	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1066	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) ≅
1067	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1068	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
1069	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1070	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
1071	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1072	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
1073	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
1074	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
1075	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1076	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1077	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1078	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
1079	  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1080	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P)) := by
1081	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
1082	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
1083	  have hηQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1084	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app Q)) := by
1085	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
1086	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
1087	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1088	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
1089	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) Q (η.app P) hηP
1090	  have hW2 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1091	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
1092	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) (a.obj P).val (η.app Q) hηQ
1093	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
1094	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
1095	  have hi2 : IsIso (a.map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
1096	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW2
1097	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1) ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi2)
1098	
1099	/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
1100	Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`
1101	of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ` unfolds to
1102	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
1103	topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
1104	`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
1105	`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.
1106	
1107	The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,
1108	the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
1109	middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂` (the form
1110	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s target carries). The two are defeq; bridging them with a local
1111	`MonoidalCategory` instance triggers a kernel-rejected diamond, so instead `φ` is defeq-cast
1112	to the outer form (`φ'`) for the `restrictScalars` factor, and the resulting composite — defeq
1113	to `pushforward φ` — is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
1114	that an eventual oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.
1115	Project-local supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
1116	noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
1117	    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
1118	    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1119	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1120	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1121	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
1122	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1123	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1124	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
1125	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
1126	  exact h
1127	
1128	/-- **The abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback is oplax monoidal**, with canonical
1129	comparison map `δ_{A,B} : f^*(A ⊗ B) ⟶ f^*A ⊗ f^*B`. This is the mate of the lax
1130	tensorator of `pushforward φ` (`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`) across the
1131	pullback–pushforward adjunction, via Mathlib's doctrinal `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. It
1132	supplies the canonical comparison map that the eventual `pullbackTensorIso` upgrades to an
1133	isomorphism — note the map exists for the *abstract* left adjoint with no sectionwise value
1134	(no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` is needed, contra the earlier reading: the comparison
1135	lives at the presheaf level where `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal). Project-local supplement;
1136	what remains Mathlib-absent is the concrete inverse-image model needed to prove `δ` is an
1137	iso (see the Phase-2 note below). -/
1138	noncomputable instance presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal
1139	    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
1140	    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1141	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1142	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1143	    [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint] :
1144	    (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).OplaxMonoidal :=
1145	  (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1146	
1147	/-! ### Phase 2 — `pullbackTensorIso` status (iter-242 finding)
1148	
1149	`pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1) is DONE (above). The remaining `pullbackTensorIso`
1150	(`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`, general `f`) is a genuine Mathlib-scale build, blocked
1151	on a Mathlib-absent ingredient confirmed this iter:

# Pending Tasks

## Lane TS — `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (+ split sub-files) — A.1.c.SubT ⊗-group law

**Status: ACTIVE — group law DONE (`picCommGroup`, iter-238); A.1.c substrate ROUTE PIVOT iter-245 —
the general strong-monoidal build (iter-244 commitment) is ABANDONED (adversarially confirmed unnecessary,
analyst loctriv-bridge `analogies/invertible-loctriv-bridge.md`). NEW route: upgrade the already-built oplax δ
`pullbackTensorMap` (DONE) to an iso ON LOCALLY-TRIVIAL pairs via `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` over a common
trivialising cover, reducing to `pullbackUnitIso` on units. Sub-lemmas D1'–D4'
(`pullbackTensorMap_natural`, `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso`, `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` [D3', the sole
new sub-step, analog of PROVEN `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`], `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`) →
`IsInvertible.pullback` (now loc-triv-hypothesised). ~8–16 iters / ~200–400 LOC (vs 20–38 for the abandoned
build). iter-244 D1 (`pullbackLanDecomposition`) RETAINED off-path. The forward bridge
`IsInvertible⟹IsLocallyTrivial` is Mathlib-scale AND off-path (consumer `OnProduct` carries IsLocallyTrivial).
Blueprint `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality` rewritten + gate this iter. RPF stays on IsLocallyTrivial
(iter-244 carrier-pivot RETRACTED); its `addCommGroup` (RelPicFunctor.lean:269) consumes the loc-triv
comparison iso (`map_add`) — opens once D1'–D4' land.**

The group `Pic X` is carried on tensor-invertibility `IsInvertible M := ∃N, M⊗N≅𝒪` (Stacks 0B8M/01CX):
the inverse is the membership WITNESS (free), so the entire dual / internal-hom / `dual_restrict_iso` /
`exists_tensorObj_inverse` apparatus is DELETED from the critical path (deferred bridge only). Confirmed
by strategy-critic ts233 (carrier pivot sound, a strict reduction of work) + mathlib-analogist ts233.

**The one remaining bottleneck = d.2 (varying-ring stalk-tensor commutation).** `thm:pic_commgroup`
needs `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (`mul_assoc`). The presheaf associator

=== LineBundlePullback IsLocallyTrivial.pullback ===
`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
Picard functor at `T`.

iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }

/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
sheaf of modules. -/
abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1

/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2

/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.

The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
  intro y
  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
  refine ⟨?_⟩
  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
  let i1 :=
    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7

/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection

For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.

Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
01HH). -/

end LaxMonoidal

/-- A functor `F : C ⥤ D` between monoidal categories is oplax monoidal if it is
equipped with morphisms `η : F.obj (𝟙_ C) ⟶ 𝟙 _D` and `δ X Y : F.obj (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y`,
satisfying the appropriate coherences. -/
@[ext]
class OplaxMonoidal (F : C ⥤ D) where
  /-- the counit morphism of a lax monoidal functor -/
  η (F) : F.obj (𝟙_ C) ⟶ 𝟙_ D
  /-- the cotensorator of an oplax monoidal functor -/
  δ (F) : ∀ X Y : C, F.obj (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y
  δ_natural_left (F) :
    ∀ {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (X' : C),
      δ X X' ≫ F.map f ▷ F.obj X' = F.map (f ▷ X') ≫ δ Y X' := by
    cat_disch
  δ_natural_right (F) :
    ∀ {X Y : C} (X' : C) (f : X ⟶ Y),
      δ X' X ≫ F.obj X' ◁ F.map f = F.map (X' ◁ f) ≫ δ X' Y := by
    cat_disch
  /-- associativity of the tensorator -/
  oplax_associativity (F) :
    ∀ X Y Z : C,
      δ (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ δ X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom =
        F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom ≫ δ X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.obj X ◁ δ Y Z := by
    cat_disch
  -- unitality
  oplax_left_unitality (F) :
    ∀ X : C, (λ_ (F.obj X)).inv = F.map (λ_ X).inv ≫ δ (𝟙_ C) X ≫ η ▷ F.obj X := by
      cat_disch
  oplax_right_unitality (F) :
    ∀ X : C, (ρ_ (F.obj X)).inv = F.map (ρ_ X).inv ≫ δ X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.obj X ◁ η := by
      cat_disch

namespace OplaxMonoidal

attribute [reassoc (attr := simp)] δ_natural_left δ_natural_right

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
alias associativity := oplax_associativity

@[simp, reassoc]
alias left_unitality := oplax_left_unitality

@[simp, reassoc]
alias right_unitality := oplax_right_unitality

section

variable (F : C ⥤ D) [F.OplaxMonoidal]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem δ_natural {X Y X' Y' : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : X' ⟶ Y') :
    δ F X X' ≫ (F.map f ⊗ₘ F.map g) = F.map (f ⊗ₘ g) ≫ δ F Y Y' := by
  simp [tensorHom_def]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem left_unitality_hom (X : C) :
    δ F (𝟙_ C) X ≫ η F ▷ F.obj X ≫ (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom = F.map (λ_ X).hom := by
  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv, left_unitality, ← Category.assoc,
    ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, id_comp]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem right_unitality_hom (X : C) :
    δ F X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.obj X ◁ η F ≫ (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom = F.map (ρ_ X).hom := by
  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv, right_unitality, ← Category.assoc,
    ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, id_comp]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem associativity_inv (X Y Z : C) :
    δ F X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.obj X ◁ δ F Y Z ≫ (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).inv =
      F.map (α_ X Y Z).inv ≫ δ F (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ δ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z := by
  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, associativity,
    ← Category.assoc, ← F.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, F.map_id, id_comp]

@[reassoc]
lemma δ_comp_η_tensorHom {X : C} {Y : D} (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
    δ F (𝟙_ C) X ≫ (η F ⊗ₘ f) = F.map (λ_ X).hom ≫ (λ_ (F.obj X)).inv ≫ 𝟙_ D ◁ f := by
  simp [tensorHom_def]

@[reassoc]
lemma δ_comp_tensorHom_η {X : C} {Y : D} (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
    δ F X (𝟙_ C) ≫ (f ⊗ₘ η F) = F.map (ρ_ X).hom ≫ (ρ_ (F.obj X)).inv ≫ f ▷ 𝟙_ D := by
  simp [tensorHom_def']

@[reassoc]
lemma δ_comp_δ_whiskerRight (X Y Z : C) :
    δ F (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ δ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z = F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom ≫
      δ F X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.obj X ◁ δ F Y Z ≫ (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).inv := by
  rw [← associativity_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]

@[reassoc]
lemma δ_comp_whiskerLeft_δ (X Y Z : C) :
    δ F X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.obj X ◁ δ F Y Z = F.map (α_ X Y Z).inv ≫
      δ F (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ δ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom := by
  rw [associativity, ← F.map_comp_assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp]

end

@[simps]
instance id : (𝟭 C).OplaxMonoidal where
  η := 𝟙 _
  δ _ _ := 𝟙 _

section

variable (F : C ⥤ D) (G : D ⥤ E) [F.OplaxMonoidal] [G.OplaxMonoidal]

@[simps]
instance comp : (F ⋙ G).OplaxMonoidal where
  η := G.map (η F) ≫ η G
  δ X Y := G.map (δ F X Y) ≫ δ G _ _
  δ_natural_left {X Y} f X' := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, δ_natural_left, ← G.map_comp_assoc, δ_natural_left, map_comp, assoc]
  δ_natural_right _ _ := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, δ_natural_right, ← G.map_comp_assoc, δ_natural_right, map_comp, assoc]
  oplax_associativity X Y Z := by
    dsimp

(PullbackConstruction.adjunction.{v} φ).isRightAdjoint

/-- The pullback functor on sheaves of modules can be described as a composition
of the forget functor to presheaves, the pullback on presheaves of modules, and
the sheafification functor. -/
noncomputable def pullbackIso :
    pullback.{v} φ ≅
      forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
    (PullbackConstruction.adjunction φ)

section

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

/-- The pullback of (pre)sheaves of modules commutes with the sheafification. -/
noncomputable def sheafificationCompPullback :
    PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.obj) ⋙ pullback.{v} φ ≅
      PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 S.obj)).comp
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ))
    ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).comp
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)))

end

end


instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId S).symm

variable (S) in
/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
category of sheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison

The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
§`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'–D4'. -/

section LocTrivPullbackTensor

/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf δ.**

`pullbackTensorMap f M N` is the four-fold composite
`(sheafificationCompPullback).hom ≫ a_Y.map δ ≫ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom ≫ a_Y.map (tensorHom …)`.
Three of the four factors (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
and the `tensorHom` of the two `pullbackValIso`s) are isomorphisms unconditionally;
the only conditional factor is the sheafification `a_Y.map δ` of the presheaf-level
oplax comparison `δ`. Hence `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an iso whenever that
sheafified δ is an iso. This isolates the SOLE remaining content (the sheafified δ)
for D2' (unit pair) and D4' (chart-chase). Project-local. -/
lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules)
    (h : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
        (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
        (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom)
          M.val N.val))) :
    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by
  unfold pullbackTensorMap
  sorry

end LocTrivPullbackTensor

end Modules

unfold pullbackTensorMap
  extract_lets φ φ' a_Y
  -- piece 2 (the sheafified δ) is the only conditional factor — supplied by `h`.
  haveI hδ : IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)) :=
    h
  -- the `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s defaults to the `Y.ringCatSheaf.obj` carrier of the
  -- monoidal structure; pin it to the syntactic `_ ⋙ forget₂` form on which the instance is keyed.
  letI mc : MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules Y.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
    inferInstanceAs
      (MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  haveI htensor : IsIso ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom ⊗ₘ
      (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom) :=
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N))).isIso_hom
  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (now-iso) `⊗ₘ`.
  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' hδ
    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (@Functor.map_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ a_Y _ htensor)))

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (2, both axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso` (private helper, L1308) — piece 4 (the sheafified `⊗ₘ`
    of the two `pullbackValIso`s) is an iso.
  - `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (L1335) — **the reduction brick**: reduces
    iso-ness of `pullbackTensorMap f M N` to iso-ness of the single sheafified presheaf comparison
    `a_Y.map (δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)`.
- **Declarations blocked (D2', D3', D4', `IsInvertible.pullback`):** all reduce, via the new brick,
  to one sheafified-δ sub-goal; the next genuine sub-step is the **sheafification-mate bridge
  `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))`** (D2') and the δ–base-change-square coherence
  (D3'). Precise statements below + an in-file `/-! D2' onward — handoff -/` block at L1354.
- **Sorry count across file: 2 → 2** (unchanged; both pre-existing + guardrailed:
  `exists_tensorObj_inverse` L672, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` L1403). **No new sorry.**

## `isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso` (L1308)
- **Approach:** build the iso directly as `a_Y.mapIso (tensorIso (C := _ ⋙ forget₂) (forget.mapIso
  (pullbackValIso f M)) (forget.mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))` and take `.isIso_hom`. Factored to a
  **top-level** lemma because `MonoidalCategory.tensorIso (C := Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)` over the
  `forget`-mapIso arguments does NOT elaborate inside a tactic block carrying `extract_lets` locals
  (synthesizes `MonoidalCategory (… ⋙ forget₂)` fine standalone, but the `(C := …)`-coercion of the
  `Y.ringCatSheaf.obj`-typed mapIso args spuriously fails the MC search there). At top level it mirrors
  the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (L242) and compiles.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.
- **Dead end (do not retry):** inside the tactic body, neither `inferInstance` on `IsIso (e₁ ⊗ₘ e₂)`,
  nor `Functor.map_isIso a_Y _`, nor `tensor_isIso` fires — the `⊗ₘ` carrier is the def's pinned
  `_ ⋙ forget₂` form while the `forget`-mapIso isos key on the defeq `Y.ringCatSheaf.obj` form, so
  discrimination-tree keying misses. Building the iso (no `IsIso` synthesis) sidesteps this entirely.

## `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (L1335)
- **Approach:** `unfold pullbackTensorMap; extract_lets φ φ' a_Y`. The unfolded body is the 4-fold
  composite `p1 ≫ a_Y.map δ ≫ p3 ≫ p4` with `p1 = (sheafificationCompPullback).hom`,
  `p3 = (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom`, `p4 = a_Y.map (⊗ₘ of the two pullbackValIso)`. `p1`,`p3` are
  `Iso.hom` (auto-iso), `p4` via the helper above, `p2` from the hypothesis `h`. Combined with
  `IsIso.comp_isIso'` (chained explicitly — `infer_instance` on the whole composite does NOT chain
  because `p4`'s `⊗ₘ` keying misses, see above).
- **Statement subtlety:** the hypothesis `h` must pin `φ'` with its explicit type via a `letI` in the
  hypothesis type (`letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂) ⟶ (Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂)
  := f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom`), else `Functor.OplaxMonoidal (PresheafOfModules.pullback …)` is stuck
  (codomain monoidal-cat metavars). This mirrors the def's own `let φ' : … := φ.hom`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. `apply isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` was
  verified to discharge the goal to exactly `IsIso (a_Y.map (δ (pullback φ') M.val N.val))`.

## D2' `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` (NOT added — no sorry left)
- **Approach (verified to compile up to the sub-goal):** `apply
  isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` ⟹ `IsIso (a_Y.map (δ (pullback φ') 𝟙_ 𝟙_))`
  (using `(SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val = 𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂))`,
  closed by `rfl` — verified live). Mathlib `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom` (in
  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean`, verified to exist) rewrites the unit-pair δ via
  `η (pullback φ')`, `λ_`, and `F.map (λ_ 𝟙_)`.
- **Genuine remaining sub-step:** `IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback
  φ')))` — the sheafified presheaf unit comparison. Morally equals the sheaf-level `pullbackUnitIso`
  (`f^*𝒪 ≅ 𝒪`, axiom-clean, L1045) through `pullbackValIso` + the sheafification counit, but that
  identification is itself a sheafification-mate bridge (the unit-side analog of the PROVEN
  `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`, L902) and is NOT yet built.
- **Result:** NOT ADDED. Next step: build the bridge lemma
  `a_Y.map (η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≅ (reconciliation) ≫ pullbackUnitIso` (or its `IsIso`
  form). Estimated ~60–120 LOC of mate calculus.

## D3' `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (NOT added)
- The sole genuinely-new sub-step: the sheafified δ commutes with the open-immersion base-change
  square `gᵢ : f⁻¹Uᵢ → Uᵢ`. Analog of the PROVEN `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (L902, mate calculus
  via `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` + `unit_conjugateEquiv`). Mathlib `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp_δ`
  exists (`Functor.lean` L347). With the reduction brick the whole chart-chase stays inside
  `IsIso (a_Y.map δ …)`. NOT ADDED — depends on D2's η-bridge being available to close each chart.

## D4' `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial` + `IsInvertible.pullback` (NOT added)
- Chart-chase via `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (L546) over the common trivialising cover (mirror
  `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`, `LineBundlePullback.lean:156`): D3' localises the sheafified δ, D1'
  (naturality of `pullbackTensorMap`) transports to the unit pair, D2' closes. Then
  `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` upgrades to `IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N)`,
  giving the iso `f^*(M⊗N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`; `IsInvertible.pullback` is the 3-line corollary.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 2 axiom-clean declarations added — `isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso`
  (L1308) and `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (L1335). The latter is precisely the
  foundation the objectives requested ("isolates the SOLE remaining content — the sheafified δ — for
  D2' and D4'"): every downstream target (D2', D3', D4', `IsInvertible.pullback`) now reduces to a
